title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on How Does the Group Gratuity Scheme Benefit You? – Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: group gratuity scheme, group gratuity scheme for employees, group gratuity plan
Body Content:
Every work organisation has certain legal and financial obligations towards its employees. Gratuity happens to be one such obligation that the employer must meet for the years of dedicated service an employee provides to the organisation.
While many of us might have heard of the term ‘gratuity’, we often remain unaware of the far-reaching implications of this term. Gratuity refers to the severance pay an employee is entitled to receive when leaving an organisation.
However, as the organisation keeps growing, keeping a track of the gratuity payments and funds becomes difficult. This is where group gratuity schemes become important. Whether you’re an employer or an employee, it is prudent to know the benefits of such schemes.
What is a Group Gratuity Scheme?
As per the Gratuity Act of 1972, if an employee has been working for an organisation for a minimum of five consecutive years and fulfils the other criteria listed in the Act, she/he will be entitled to a lump sum payment when separating from the organisation.
However, as the business keeps growing, maintaining a set reserve for gratuity funds always can become quite challenging. Hence, most organisations opt for group gratuity schemes for employees.
How Do Group Gratuity Schemes Work?
Group gratuity plans operate in a fairly simple way. Every month the employer deposits a certain amount of money that’s kept aside for meeting the gratuity liabilities of the firm. With the returns on these investments, over time, a sizable corpus may be built against which claims can be filed, as and when employees exit the firm. A gratuity is a retirement planning bonus paid to an employee in exchange for services rendered. After 5 years of service with the organisation, an employee is eligible for a retirement incentive.
How do Group Gratuity Schemes Benefit You?
Group gratuity plans offer an extensive range of benefits that make them prudent and safe options to consider. Here’s a look at the benefits offered by such plans for both employers and employees-
For employers:
Freedom from Financial Worries- As an employer, if you fail to invest in such group gratuity schemes, you may either must borrow or dig into your own personal savings to meet your gratuity liabilities. However, if you opt for a group gratuity scheme for your employees, you can save up a sizable corpus over time to avoid the financial burden of making lump sum payments from the working capital or liquid assets of the firm.
Flexibility to Choose Funds- As per their risk appetite, employers can choose from an extensive range of market-linked funds to build their gratuity corpus. Group gratuity funds also allow the policyholder (employer) to switch between funds as the market changes.
Tax Benefits- Group gratuity plans also offer tax rebates to employers since the money that’s invested in such plans is considered a business expense.
For employees:
Maturity Benefits- Employees covered by a group gratuity policy are subject to receive the lump sum maturity benefit when they retire or resign. This helps safeguard the foreseeable future of employees, freeing them from anxieties about the piling expenses.
Death Benefits- if an employee passes away during her/his tenure of service, the benefit from the plan will be awarded to the next of kin. This death benefit pay out acts as a financial safety net for an employee’s family.
FAQs
2. How is gratuity calculated in a group gratuity plan?
The calculation is (15 * your last drew salary * the duration of your employment) / 30). For example, suppose you earn Rs 30,000 per month. You have worked for the company for seven years and the employer is not covered by the Gratuity Act. Amount of Gratuity = (15 * 30,000 * 7) / 30 = Rs 1,05,000.
3. What is the time limit for gratuity payments?
Once a gratuity liability claim is raised, the employer has 30 days to pay the same.
4. What is the minimum age for group gratuity plans?
While most plans have their own eligibility criteria, generally 18 years is considered the minimum age to qualify for such a plan, while 80 years is the upper limit.
5. How long does a group gratuity policy last?
Such policies last for a year, after which they need to be renewed.
6. When can contributions to group gratuity plans be made?
Employers enjoy considerable flexibility when making gratuity contributions to such plans. They can either pay for the same monthly, quarterly, bi-annually, or yearly, as per their convenience depending on the product opted for with its terms and conditions.
Summing It Up
If you’re an employer, then opting for a group gratuity scheme is prudent because such a plan keeps you ready to tackle future gratuity liabilities without the fear of affecting your business capital reserves. Additionally, it also helps boost the morale of your employees and build a relationship of trust and loyalty with them. As an employee, such plans ensure that you’re duly rewarded for your years in service and that the financial future of your loved ones remains safe if something untoward were to happen to you. However, it is advisable that one should consult a tax consultant before claiming any tax benefit in their return of income.
Disclaimer:
The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale.

full_pdf_text:
How Does the Group Gratuity Scheme Benefit You?
Keywords: group gratuity scheme, group gratuity scheme for employees, group gratuity plan
Every work organisation has certain legal and financial obligations towards its employees. Gratuity happens to be one such obligation that the employer must meet for the years of dedicated service an employee provides to the organisation.
While many of us might have heard of the term ‘gratuity’, we often remain unaware of the far-reaching implications of this term. Gratuity refers to the severance pay an employee is entitled to receive when leaving an organisation.
However, as the organisation keeps growing, keeping a track of the gratuity payments and funds becomes difficult. This is where group gratuity schemes become important. Whether you’re an employer or an employee, it is prudent to know the benefits of such schemes. What is a Group Gratuity Scheme?
As per the Payment of Gratuity Act of 1972, if an employee has been working for an organisation for a minimum of five consecutive years and fulfils the other criteria listed in the Act, she/he will be entitled to a lump sum payment when separating from the organisation.
However, as the business keeps growing, maintaining a set reserve for gratuity funds can become quite challenging. Hence, most organisations opt for group gratuity schemes for employees.
How Do Group Gratuity Schemes Work? Group gratuity plans work in a straightforward manner. Every month, the employer deposits a certain amount of money as gratuity balance to be used to cover the firm's gratuity commitments. This money is put into a group of market-linked funds, such as equities and debt funds. With the earnings on these investments, a significant corpus may be established over time, against which reimbursement claims can be submitted when employees leave the company. Gratuity is a kind of Loyalty Bonus paid by the employer to his employees in exchange for long-term services done. It is normally paid during retirement; however, it may be given sooner if specific requirements are satisfied. An employee is entitled for a retirement incentive after 5 years of service with the organisation. How do Group Gratuity Schemes Benefit You?
Group gratuity plans offer an extensive range of benefits that make them prudent and safe options to consider. Here’s a look at the benefits offered by such plans for both employers and employees-
For employers:
Freedom from Financial Worries- As an employer, if you fail to invest in such group gratuity schemes, you must borrow or dig into your own personal savings to meet your gratuity liabilities. However, if you opt for a group gratuity scheme for your employees, you can save up a sizable corpus over time to avoid the financial burden of making lump sum payments from the working capital or liquid assets of the firm.
Flexibility to Choose Funds- As per their risk appetite, employers can choose from an extensive range of market-linked funds to build their gratuity corpus. Group gratuity funds also allow the policyholder (employer) to switch between funds as the market condition changes. This criterion is different for non- ULIP funds varying as per employers’ regulations.
Tax Benefits- Group gratuity plans also offer tax rebates to employers as per provisions stated in Income Tax Act, 1961 since the money that’s invested in such plans is considered a business expense.
For employees:
Maturity Benefits- Employees covered by a group gratuity policy are subject to receive the lump sum maturity benefit if all the conditions mentioned in the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972 are met, when they retire or resign, or on their superannuation, or on their death or disablement due to accident or disease. This helps safeguard the foreseeable future of employees, freeing them from anxieties about the piling expenses.
Death Benefits- if an employee passes away during her/his tenure of service, the benefit from the plan will be awarded to the to their nominee or, if no nomination has been made, to their legal heirs. This death benefit pay out acts as a financial safety net for an employee’s nominees.
FAQs
2. How is gratuity calculated in a group gratuity plan?
The calculation is 15 * your last drew salary * the duration of your employment) / 30. For example, suppose you earn Rs 30,000 per month. You have worked for the company for seven years and the employer is not covered by the Gratuity Act. Amount of Gratuity = (15 * 30,000 * 7) / 30 = Rs 1,05,000.3. What is the time limit for gratuity payments?
The employer has 30 days to pay the same from the date it becomes payable subject to provisions stated in the Payment of Gratuity Act, 1972.4. How long does a group gratuity policy last? Such policies last for a year, after which they need to be renewed. 5. When can contributions to group gratuity plans be made? Employers enjoy considerable flexibility when making gratuity contributions to such plans. They can either pay for the same monthly, quarterly, bi-annually, or yearly, as per their convenience depending on the product opted for with its terms and conditions. Summing It Up
If you’re an employer, then opting for a group gratuity scheme is prudent because such a plan keeps you ready to tackle future gratuity liabilities without the fear of affecting your business capital reserves. Additionally, it also helps boost the morale of your employees and build a relationship of trust and loyalty with them. As an employee, such plans ensure that you’re duly rewarded for your years in service and that the financial future of your loved ones remains safe if something untoward were to happen to you. However, it is advisable that one should consult a tax consultant before claiming any tax benefit in their return of income.
Disclaimer: The views stated in this article is not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
